objective: Fix execution-runner timeout retry state so a successful retry is reported as succeeded and the timeout model is safe to push to main.
scope: focused verification for timeout, fake-provider, and headless parallel execution
out_of_scope: Changing provider invocation semantics beyond timeout/retry state handling
Reworking the timeout configuration model already present in the current diff
Broad refactors outside the execution runner and focused regression coverage
constraints: Use delegated-implementation with parent-owned plan, integration, and final verification
Do not revert unrelated uncommitted changes already present in the tree
Keep worker write scope bounded and avoid overlapping edits
assumptions: The known blocker is caused by timeout state persisting after a later attempt exits successfully
The polling loop may mark timeout after the process exits during the sleep interval unless HasExited is rechecked
A focused regression can reproduce a first-attempt timeout followed by a second-attempt success without requiring long-running external providers

tasks.title: Cover retry success regression
tasks.intent: Add a stable regression test for the exact main-blocking case discovered in review.
acceptance: The test demonstrates the current broken behavior before the runner fix when run by the parent
The test uses local PowerShell scripts and temporary files only
The test asserts returned result_status=succeeded, failure_class=null, exit_code=0, timed_out=false, and was_escalated=false for a first-attempt timeout followed by retry success
The test asserts persisted job metadata status=finished, result_status=succeeded, failure_class=null, exit_code=0, timed_out=false, and was_escalated=false for that final successful job
The test asserts the matching job.finished event records result_status=succeeded, failure_class=null, and exit_code=0
The test does not add significant runtime beyond the existing regression suite
risks: Timing-sensitive tests can be flaky if sleep durations are too tight
The test can accidentally depend on wall-clock timing rather than runner state

tasks.title: Repair timeout retry state
tasks.intent: Ensure timeout is attempt-local for control flow while final job metadata reflects the final normalized result.
acceptance: A provider that times out on attempt 1 and succeeds on attempt 2 returns result_status=succeeded
The final returned result for that scenario has failure_class=null, exit_code=0, timed_out=false, and was_escalated=false
The persisted job metadata for that scenario has status=finished, result_status=succeeded, failure_class=null, exit_code=0, timed_out=false, and was_escalated=false
The matching job.finished event for that scenario records result_status=succeeded, failure_class=null, and exit_code=0
A provider that times out on all allowed attempts still fails with failure_class=timeout, timed_out=true, and was_escalated=true
risks: Clearing timeout state too broadly could hide a final timeout failure
Changing process wait behavior could reintroduce hangs or lose stdout/stderr
Metadata and job.finished events can diverge from returned results if only one surface is normalized

verification: pwsh -NoLogo -NoProfile -Command parse check for touched .ps1 files
